revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:郡戸王子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:郡戸王子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:郡戸王子]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11643221]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:50, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kōto Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]].
==Overview== [[File:Kozu-Gu Himekoso-jinja Koto-oji.JPG|thumb|280px|{{ill|Himekoso Shrine|ja|比売古曽神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himekoso Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Kōzu-gū|en|Kōzu-gū|WD=Q11671792|lt=Kōzu-gū}}, believed to be the site of Kōto Ōji]] '''Kōto Ōji''' ('''郡戸王子''') was the third {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrine along the {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|WD=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō}} pilgrimage route. The shrine no longer exists. The site is believed to be where {{ill|Kōzu-gū|en|Kōzu-gū|WD=Q11671792|lt=Kōzu-gū}} is currently located, in {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|en|Chūō-ku, Osaka|WD=Q876485|lt=Chūō-ku, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka|en|Osaka|WD=Q35765|lt=Osaka City}} (1-1-29 Kōzu), though there is no definitive proof.
According to the historical text ''{{ill|Settsu-shi|ja|摂津志|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Settsu-shi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五畿内志|1=Five Kinai Chronicles|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11372555}}'', the {{ill|Himekoso Shrine|ja|比売許曽神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himekoso Shrine|qq=}}, which once stood at the current location of Kōzu Shrine, was regarded as the Kōto Ōji.
== Nearest Station == * {{ill|Tanimachi Kyūchōme Station|en|Tanimachi Kyūchōme Station|WD=Q1041977|lt=Tanimachi Kyūchōme Station}} on the {{ill|Tanimachi Line|en|Tanimachi Line|WD=Q1142773|lt=Osaka Metro Tanimachi Line}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|WD=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō}} – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji Route|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} : {{ill|Sakaguchi Ōji|ja|坂口王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaguchi Ōji|1=Sakaguchi Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11425069}} – '''Kōto Ōji''' – {{ill|Ueno Ōji (Osaka)|ja|上野王子 (大阪市)|lt=Ueno Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueno Ōji (Osaka)|1=Ueno Ōji Shrine (Osaka)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11360400}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:郡戸王子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:郡戸王子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:郡戸王子]]
'''Kōto Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]].
==Overview== [[File:Kozu-Gu Himekoso-jinja Koto-oji.JPG|thumb|280px|{{ill|Himekoso Shrine|ja|比売古曽神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himekoso Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Kōzu-gū|en|Kōzu-gū|WD=Q11671792|lt=Kōzu-gū}}, believed to be the site of Kōto Ōji]] '''Kōto Ōji''' ('''郡戸王子''') was the third {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrine along the {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|WD=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō}} pilgrimage route. The shrine no longer exists. The site is believed to be where {{ill|Kōzu-gū|en|Kōzu-gū|WD=Q11671792|lt=Kōzu-gū}} is currently located, in {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|en|Chūō-ku, Osaka|WD=Q876485|lt=Chūō-ku, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka|en|Osaka|WD=Q35765|lt=Osaka City}} (1-1-29 Kōzu), though there is no definitive proof.
According to the historical text ''{{ill|Settsu-shi|ja|摂津志|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Settsu-shi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五畿内志|1=Five Kinai Chronicles|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11372555}}'', the {{ill|Himekoso Shrine|ja|比売許曽神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himekoso Shrine|qq=}}, which once stood at the current location of Kōzu Shrine, was regarded as the Kōto Ōji.
== Nearest Station == * {{ill|Tanimachi Kyūchōme Station|en|Tanimachi Kyūchōme Station|WD=Q1041977|lt=Tanimachi Kyūchōme Station}} on the {{ill|Tanimachi Line|en|Tanimachi Line|WD=Q1142773|lt=Osaka Metro Tanimachi Line}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|WD=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō}} – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji Route|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} : {{ill|Sakaguchi Ōji|ja|坂口王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaguchi Ōji|1=Sakaguchi Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11425069}} – '''Kōto Ōji''' – {{ill|Ueno Ōji (Osaka)|ja|上野王子 (大阪市)|lt=Ueno Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueno Ōji (Osaka)|1=Ueno Ōji Shrine (Osaka)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11360400}}

'''Kōto Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]].
==Overview== [[File:Kozu-Gu Himekoso-jinja Koto-oji.JPG|thumb|280px|{{ill|Himekoso Shrine|ja|比売古曽神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himekoso Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Kōzu-gū|en|Kōzu-gū|WD=Q11671792|lt=Kōzu-gū}}, believed to be the site of Kōto Ōji]] '''Kōto Ōji''' ('''郡戸王子''') was the third {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrine along the {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|WD=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō}} pilgrimage route. The shrine no longer exists. The site is believed to be where {{ill|Kōzu-gū|en|Kōzu-gū|WD=Q11671792|lt=Kōzu-gū}} is currently located, in {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|en|Chūō-ku, Osaka|WD=Q876485|lt=Chūō-ku, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka|en|Osaka|WD=Q35765|lt=Osaka City}} (1-1-29 Kōzu), though there is no definitive proof.
According to the historical text ''{{ill|Settsu-shi|ja|摂津志|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Settsu-shi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五畿内志|1=Five Kinai Chronicles|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11372555}}'', the {{ill|Himekoso Shrine|ja|比売許曽神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himekoso Shrine|qq=}}, which once stood at the current location of Kōzu Shrine, was regarded as the Kōto Ōji.
== Nearest Station == * {{ill|Tanimachi Kyūchōme Station|en|Tanimachi Kyūchōme Station|WD=Q1041977|lt=Tanimachi Kyūchōme Station}} on the {{ill|Tanimachi Line|en|Tanimachi Line|WD=Q1142773|lt=Osaka Metro Tanimachi Line}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|WD=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō}} – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji Route|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} : {{ill|Sakaguchi Ōji|ja|坂口王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaguchi Ōji|1=Sakaguchi Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11425069}} – '''Kōto Ōji''' – {{ill|Ueno Ōji (Osaka)|ja|上野王子 (大阪市)|lt=Ueno Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueno Ōji (Osaka)|1=Ueno Ōji Shrine (Osaka)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11360400}}

The shrine no longer exists. The site is believed to be where {{ill|Kōzu-gū|en|Kōzu-gū|WD=Q11671792|lt=Kōzu-gū}} is currently located, in {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|en|Chūō-ku, Osaka|WD=Q876485|lt=Chūō-ku, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka|en|Osaka|WD=Q35765|lt=Osaka City}} (1-1-29 Kōzu), though there is no definitive proof.
According to the historical text ''{{ill|Settsu-shi|ja|摂津志|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Settsu-shi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五畿内志|1=Five Kinai Chronicles|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11372555}}'', the {{ill|Himekoso Shrine|ja|比売許曽神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himekoso Shrine|qq=}}, which once stood at the current location of Kōzu Shrine, was regarded as the Kōto Ōji.
== Nearest Station == * {{ill|Tanimachi Kyūchōme Station|en|Tanimachi Kyūchōme Station|WD=Q1041977|lt=Tanimachi Kyūchōme Station}} on the {{ill|Tanimachi Line|en|Tanimachi Line|WD=Q1142773|lt=Osaka Metro Tanimachi Line}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:郡戸王子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:郡戸王子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:郡戸王子]]
'''Kōto Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]].
==Overview== [[File:Kozu-Gu Himekoso-jinja Koto-oji.JPG|thumb|280px|{{ill|Himekoso Shrine|ja|比売古曽神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himekoso Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Kōzu-gū|en|Kōzu-gū|WD=Q11671792|lt=Kōzu-gū}}, believed to be the site of Kōto Ōji]] '''Kōto Ōji''' ('''郡戸王子''') was the third {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrine along the {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|WD=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō}} pilgrimage route. The shrine no longer exists. The site is believed to be where {{ill|Kōzu-gū|en|Kōzu-gū|WD=Q11671792|lt=Kōzu-gū}} is currently located, in {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|en|Chūō-ku, Osaka|WD=Q876485|lt=Chūō-ku, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka|en|Osaka|WD=Q35765|lt=Osaka City}} (1-1-29 Kōzu), though there is no definitive proof.
According to the historical text ''{{ill|Settsu-shi|ja|摂津志|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Settsu-shi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五畿内志|1=Five Kinai Chronicles|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11372555}}'', the {{ill|Himekoso Shrine|ja|比売許曽神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himekoso Shrine|qq=}}, which once stood at the current location of Kōzu Shrine, was regarded as the Kōto Ōji.
== Nearest Station == * {{ill|Tanimachi Kyūchōme Station|en|Tanimachi Kyūchōme Station|WD=Q1041977|lt=Tanimachi Kyūchōme Station}} on the {{ill|Tanimachi Line|en|Tanimachi Line|WD=Q1142773|lt=Osaka Metro Tanimachi Line}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|WD=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō}} – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji Route|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} : {{ill|Sakaguchi Ōji|ja|坂口王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaguchi Ōji|1=Sakaguchi Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11425069}} – '''Kōto Ōji''' – {{ill|Ueno Ōji (Osaka)|ja|上野王子 (大阪市)|lt=Ueno Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueno Ōji (Osaka)|1=Ueno Ōji Shrine (Osaka)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11360400}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:郡戸王子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:郡戸王子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:郡戸王子]]
'''Kōto Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]].
==Overview== [[File:Kozu-Gu Himekoso-jinja Koto-oji.JPG|thumb|280px|{{ill|Himekoso Shrine|ja|比売古曽神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himekoso Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Kōzu-gū|en|Kōzu-gū|WD=Q11671792|lt=Kōzu-gū|1=Kōzu-gū}}, believed to be the site of Kōto Ōji]] '''Kōto Ōji''' ('''郡戸王子''') was the third {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrine along the {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|WD=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō|1=Kumano Kodō}} pilgrimage route. The shrine no longer exists. The site is believed to be where {{ill|Kōzu-gū|en|Kōzu-gū|WD=Q11671792|lt=Kōzu-gū|1=Kōzu-gū}} is currently located, in {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|en|Chūō-ku, Osaka|WD=Q876485|lt=Chūō-ku, Osaka|1=Chūō-ku, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka|en|Osaka|WD=Q35765|lt=Osaka City|1=Osaka}} (1-1-29 Kōzu), though there is no definitive proof.
According to the historical text ''{{ill|Settsu-shi|ja|摂津志|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Settsu-shi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五畿内志|1=Five Kinai Chronicles|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11372555}}'', the {{ill|Himekoso Shrine|ja|比売許曽神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himekoso Shrine|qq=}}, which once stood at the current location of Kōzu Shrine, was regarded as the Kōto Ōji.
== Nearest Station == * {{ill|Tanimachi Kyūchōme Station|en|Tanimachi Kyūchōme Station|WD=Q1041977|lt=Tanimachi Kyūchōme Station|1=Tanimachi Kyūchōme Station}} on the {{ill|Tanimachi Line|en|Tanimachi Line|WD=Q1142773|lt=Osaka Metro Tanimachi Line|1=Tanimachi Line}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|WD=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō|1=Kumano Kodō}} – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji Route|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} : {{ill|Sakaguchi Ōji|ja|坂口王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaguchi Ōji|1=Sakaguchi Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11425069}} – '''Kōto Ōji''' – {{ill|Ueno Ōji (Osaka)|ja|上野王子 (大阪市)|lt=Ueno Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueno Ōji (Osaka)|1=Ueno Ōji Shrine (Osaka)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11360400}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:郡戸王子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:郡戸王子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:郡戸王子]]
'''Kōto Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]].
==Overview== [[File:Kozu-Gu Himekoso-jinja Koto-oji.JPG|thumb|280px|{{ill|Himekoso Shrine|ja|比売古曽神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himekoso Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Kōzu-gū|en|Kōzu-gū|WD=Q11671792|lt=Kōzu-gū|1=Kōzu-gū}}, believed to be the site of Kōto Ōji]] '''Kōto Ōji''' ('''郡戸王子''') was the third {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrine along the {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|WD=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō|1=Kumano Kodō}} pilgrimage route. The shrine no longer exists. The site is believed to be where {{ill|Kōzu-gū|en|Kōzu-gū|WD=Q11671792|lt=Kōzu-gū|1=Kōzu-gū}} is currently located, in {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|en|Chūō-ku, Osaka|WD=Q876485|lt=Chūō-ku, Osaka|1=Chūō-ku, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka|en|Osaka|WD=Q35765|lt=Osaka City|1=Osaka}} (1-1-29 Kōzu), though there is no definitive proof.
According to the historical text ''{{ill|Settsu-shi|ja|摂津志|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Settsu-shi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五畿内志|1=Five Kinai Chronicles|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11372555}}'', the {{ill|Himekoso Shrine|ja|比売許曽神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himekoso Shrine|qq=}}, which once stood at the current location of Kōzu Shrine, was regarded as the Kōto Ōji.
== Nearest Station == * {{ill|Tanimachi Kyūchōme Station|en|Tanimachi Kyūchōme Station|WD=Q1041977|lt=Tanimachi Kyūchōme Station|1=Tanimachi Kyūchōme Station}} on the {{ill|Tanimachi Line|en|Tanimachi Line|WD=Q1142773|lt=Osaka Metro Tanimachi Line|1=Tanimachi Line}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|WD=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō|1=Kumano Kodō}} – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji Route|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} : {{ill|Sakaguchi Ōji|ja|坂口王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaguchi Ōji|1=Sakaguchi Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11425069}} – '''Kōto Ōji''' – {{ill|Ueno Ōji (Osaka)|ja|上野王子 (大阪市)|lt=Ueno Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueno Ōji (Osaka)|1=Ueno Ōji Shrine (Osaka)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11360400}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:郡戸王子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:郡戸王子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:郡戸王子]]
'''Kōto Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]].
==Overview== [[File:Kozu-Gu Himekoso-jinja Koto-oji.JPG|thumb|280px|{{ill|Himekoso Shrine|ja|比売古曽神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himekoso Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Kōzu-gū|en|Kōzu-gū|WD=Q11671792|lt=Kōzu-gū|1=Kōzu-gū}}, believed to be the site of Kōto Ōji]] '''Kōto Ōji''' ('''郡戸王子''') was the third {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrine along the {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|WD=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō|1=Kumano Kodō}} pilgrimage route. The shrine no longer exists. The site is believed to be where {{ill|Kōzu-gū|en|Kōzu-gū|WD=Q11671792|lt=Kōzu-gū|1=Kōzu-gū}} is currently located, in {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|en|Chūō-ku, Osaka|WD=Q876485|lt=Chūō-ku, Osaka|1=Chūō-ku, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka|en|Osaka|WD=Q35765|lt=Osaka City|1=Osaka}} (1-1-29 Kōzu), though there is no definitive proof.
According to the historical text ''{{ill|Settsu-shi|ja|摂津志|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Settsu-shi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五畿内志|1=Five Kinai Chronicles|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11372555}}'', the {{ill|Himekoso Shrine|ja|比売許曽神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himekoso Shrine|qq=}}, which once stood at the current location of Kōzu Shrine, was regarded as the Kōto Ōji.
== Nearest Station == * {{ill|Tanimachi Kyūchōme Station|en|Tanimachi Kyūchōme Station|WD=Q1041977|lt=Tanimachi Kyūchōme Station|1=Tanimachi Kyūchōme Station}} on the {{ill|Tanimachi Line|en|Tanimachi Line|WD=Q1142773|lt=Osaka Metro Tanimachi Line|1=Tanimachi Line}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|WD=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō|1=Kumano Kodō}} – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji Route|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} : {{ill|Sakaguchi Ōji|ja|坂口王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaguchi Ōji|1=Sakaguchi Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11425069}} – '''Kōto Ōji''' – {{ill|Ueno Ōji (Osaka)|ja|上野王子 (大阪市)|lt=Ueno Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueno Ōji (Osaka)|1=Ueno Ōji Shrine (Osaka)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11360400}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:郡戸王子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:郡戸王子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:郡戸王子]]
'''Kōto Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]].
==Overview== [[File:Kozu-Gu Himekoso-jinja Koto-oji.JPG|thumb|280px|{{ill|Himekoso Shrine|ja|比売古曽神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himekoso Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Kōzu-gū|en|Kōzu-gū|WD=Q11671792|lt=Kōzu-gū|1=Kōzu-gū}}, believed to be the site of Kōto Ōji]] '''Kōto Ōji''' ('''郡戸王子''') was the third {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrine along the {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|WD=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō|1=Kumano Kodō}} pilgrimage route. The shrine no longer exists. The site is believed to be where {{ill|Kōzu-gū|en|Kōzu-gū|WD=Q11671792|lt=Kōzu-gū|1=Kōzu-gū}} is currently located, in {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|en|Chūō-ku, Osaka|WD=Q876485|lt=Chūō-ku, Osaka|1=Chūō-ku, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka|en|Osaka|WD=Q35765|lt=Osaka City|1=Osaka}} (1-1-29 Kōzu), though there is no definitive proof.
According to the historical text ''{{ill|Settsu-shi|ja|摂津志|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Settsu-shi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五畿内志|1=Five Kinai Chronicles|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11372555}}'', the {{ill|Himekoso Shrine|ja|比売許曽神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himekoso Shrine|qq=}}, which once stood at the current location of Kōzu Shrine, was regarded as the Kōto Ōji.
== Nearest Station == * {{ill|Tanimachi Kyūchōme Station|en|Tanimachi Kyūchōme Station|WD=Q1041977|lt=Tanimachi Kyūchōme Station|1=Tanimachi Kyūchōme Station}} on the {{ill|Tanimachi Line|en|Tanimachi Line|WD=Q1142773|lt=Osaka Metro Tanimachi Line|1=Tanimachi Line}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|WD=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō|1=Kumano Kodō}} – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji Route|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} : {{ill|Sakaguchi Ōji|ja|坂口王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaguchi Ōji|1=Sakaguchi Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11425069}} – '''Kōto Ōji''' – {{ill|Ueno Ōji (Osaka)|ja|上野王子 (大阪市)|lt=Ueno Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueno Ōji (Osaka)|1=Ueno Ōji Shrine (Osaka)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11360400}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:郡戸王子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:郡戸王子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:郡戸王子]]
'''Kōto Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]].
==Overview== [[File:Kozu-Gu Himekoso-jinja Koto-oji.JPG|thumb|280px|{{ill|Himekoso Shrine|ja|比売古曽神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himekoso Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Kōzu-gū|en|Kōzu-gū|WD=Q11671792|lt=Kōzu-gū|1=Kōzu-gū}}, believed to be the site of Kōto Ōji]] '''Kōto Ōji''' ('''郡戸王子''') was the third {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrine along the {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|WD=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō|1=Kumano Kodō}} pilgrimage route. The shrine no longer exists. The site is believed to be where {{ill|Kōzu-gū|en|Kōzu-gū|WD=Q11671792|lt=Kōzu-gū|1=Kōzu-gū}} is currently located, in {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|en|Chūō-ku, Osaka|WD=Q876485|lt=Chūō-ku, Osaka|1=Chūō-ku, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka|en|Osaka|WD=Q35765|lt=Osaka City|1=Osaka}} (1-1-29 Kōzu), though there is no definitive proof.
According to the historical text ''{{ill|Settsu-shi|ja|摂津志|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Settsu-shi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五畿内志|1=Five Kinai Chronicles|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11372555}}'', the {{ill|Himekoso Shrine|ja|比売許曽神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himekoso Shrine|qq=}}, which once stood at the current location of Kōzu Shrine, was regarded as the Kōto Ōji.
== Nearest Station == * {{ill|Tanimachi Kyūchōme Station|en|Tanimachi Kyūchōme Station|WD=Q1041977|lt=Tanimachi Kyūchōme Station|1=Tanimachi Kyūchōme Station}} on the {{ill|Tanimachi Line|en|Tanimachi Line|WD=Q1142773|lt=Osaka Metro Tanimachi Line|1=Tanimachi Line}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|WD=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō|1=Kumano Kodō}} – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji Route|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} : {{ill|Sakaguchi Ōji|ja|坂口王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaguchi Ōji|1=Sakaguchi Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11425069}} – '''Kōto Ōji''' – {{ill|Ueno Ōji (Osaka)|ja|上野王子 (大阪市)|lt=Ueno Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueno Ōji (Osaka)|1=Ueno Ōji Shrine (Osaka)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11360400}}

'''Kōto Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]].
==Overview== [[File:Kozu-Gu Himekoso-jinja Koto-oji.JPG|thumb|280px|{{ill|Himekoso Shrine|ja|比売古曽神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himekoso Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Kōzu-gū|en|Kōzu-gū|qid=Q11671792|lt=Kōzu-gū|1=Kōzu-gū}}, believed to be the site of Kōto Ōji]] '''Kōto Ōji''' ('''郡戸王子''') was the third {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrine along the {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|qid=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō|1=Kumano Kodō}} pilgrimage route. The shrine no longer exists. The site is believed to be where {{ill|Kōzu-gū|en|Kōzu-gū|qid=Q11671792|lt=Kōzu-gū|1=Kōzu-gū}} is currently located, in {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|en|Chūō-ku, Osaka|qid=Q876485|lt=Chūō-ku, Osaka|1=Chūō-ku, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka|en|Osaka|qid=Q35765|lt=Osaka City|1=Osaka}} (1-1-29 Kōzu), though there is no definitive proof.
According to the historical text ''{{ill|Settsu-shi|ja|摂津志|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Settsu-shi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五畿内志|1=Five Kinai Chronicles|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11372555}}'', the {{ill|Himekoso Shrine|ja|比売許曽神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himekoso Shrine|qq=}}, which once stood at the current location of Kōzu Shrine, was regarded as the Kōto Ōji.
== Nearest Station == * {{ill|Tanimachi Kyūchōme Station|en|Tanimachi Kyūchōme Station|qid=Q1041977|lt=Tanimachi Kyūchōme Station|1=Tanimachi Kyūchōme Station}} on the {{ill|Tanimachi Line|en|Tanimachi Line|qid=Q1142773|lt=Osaka Metro Tanimachi Line|1=Tanimachi Line}}
